req.title: Allergy intolerance (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-034 covers three orthogonal risk axes: (1) happy-path allergy creation and downstream alert generation, (2) duplicate-detection returning 409, and (3) timestamp/numeric boundary behaviour. EP covers the valid allergy payload partition; NEG covers the duplicate-conflict partition; BVA covers the timestamp precision edge (onset at epoch-zero) and a numeric severity at its minimum legal value. STC covers the state transition from 'no allergy recorded' through 'allergy active' to 'MedicationRequest conflict-alert generated'. DT is applied to the conflict-rule table (allergy present vs absent × medication present vs absent). Each test is self-contained via a unique patient fixture created inline and deleted in teardown, making them idempotent.

tests.name: test_create_allergy_intolerance_happy_path
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <fixture: clinician_token>
A unique patient resource exists via <helper_fn: create_patient()>
No prior AllergyIntolerance exists for this patient
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with valid payload referencing patient id
Assert HTTP 201 and resource id returned
expected: GET returns the same resource with status 'active'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_create_allergy_intolerance_happy_path(clinician_token, create_patient, cleanup_resource):
    patient_id = create_patient(clinician_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]},
        "onsetDateTime": "2023-06-15T10:00:00Z"
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    resource_id = resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance/{resource_id}", headers)
    assert resp.json()["resourceType"] == "AllergyIntolerance"
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance/{resource_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["clinicalStatus"]["coding"][0]["code"] == "active"

tests.name: test_duplicate_allergy_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <fixture: clinician_token>
A unique patient resource exists via <helper_fn: create_patient()>
Identical AllergyIntolerance already posted (same patient, code, onset)
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome describing the duplicate
trace: R-HLT-034: error pathway - 409 when exact duplicate
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_duplicate_allergy_returns_409(clinician_token, create_patient, cleanup_resource):
    patient_id = create_patient(clinician_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]},
        "onsetDateTime": "2023-06-15T10:00:00Z"
    }
    first = requests.post(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201, f"Setup failed: {first.status_code}"
    resource_id = first.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance/{resource_id}", headers)
    second = requests.post(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {second.status_code}: {second.text}"
    body = second.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_allergy_onset_at_unix_epoch_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <fixture: clinician_token>
A unique patient resource exists via <helper_fn: create_patient()>
No prior AllergyIntolerance for this patient
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with onsetDateTime set to Unix epoch '1970-01-01T00:00:00Z' (minimum legal FHIR dateTime)
expected: No server-side truncation or rejection of the epoch timestamp
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
EPOCH = "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"

def test_allergy_onset_at_unix_epoch_boundary(clinician_token, create_patient, cleanup_resource):
    patient_id = create_patient(clinician_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "1191", "display": "Aspirin"}]},
        "onsetDateTime": EPOCH
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    resource_id = resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance/{resource_id}", headers)
    stored = resp.json().get("onsetDateTime", "")
    assert stored.startswith("1970-01-01"), f"Epoch onset not preserved: {stored}"

tests.name: test_medication_request_triggers_conflict_alert_after_allergy
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <fixture: clinician_token>
A unique patient resource exists via <helper_fn: create_patient()>
AllergyIntolerance for Penicillin already recorded for this patient
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance for Penicillin to transition patient state to 'has active allergy'
POST /MedicationRequest for Amoxicillin (Penicillin-class) for the same patient
GET /MedicationRequest/{id} or check response for conflict warning extension
expected: MedicationRequest POST succeeds (201 or 200) but response contains a conflict alert or detectedIssue reference
trace: R-HLT-034: subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_medication_request_triggers_conflict_alert_after_allergy(clinician_token, create_patient, cleanup_resource):
    patient_id = create_patient(clinician_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    allergy_payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]}
    }
    a_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance", json=allergy_payload, headers=headers)
    assert a_resp.status_code == 201
    allergy_id = a_resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance/{allergy_id}", headers)
    med_payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "723", "display": "Amoxicillin"}]}
    }
    m_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/MedicationRequest", json=med_payload, headers=headers)
    assert m_resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"MedRequest failed: {m_resp.status_code}"
    med_id = m_resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource(f"{BASE}/MedicationRequest/{med_id}", headers)
    body = m_resp.json()
    has_alert = (
        any("detectedIssue" in str(ext) for ext in body.get("extension", []))
        or "detectedIssue" in body
        or body.get("_conflictAlert") is not None
    )
    assert has_alert, f"No conflict alert in MedicationRequest response: {body}"

tests.name: test_allergy_numeric_severity_minimum_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <fixture: clinician_token>
A unique patient resource exists via <helper_fn: create_patient()>
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with reaction.severity set to 'mild' (lowest valid FHIR code) and criticality set to 'low'
Verify severity and criticality are stored exactly as submitted
trace: R-HLT-034: boundary cases on numeric elements
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_allergy_numeric_severity_minimum_boundary(clinician_token, create_patient, cleanup_resource):
    patient_id = create_patient(clinician_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "criticality": "low",
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "2670", "display": "Codeine"}]},
        "reaction": [{"manifestation": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "271807003", "display": "Rash"}]}], "severity": "mild"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    resource_id = body["id"]
    cleanup_resource(f"{BASE}/AllergyIntolerance/{resource_id}", headers)
    assert body.get("criticality") == "low", f"criticality mismatch: {body.get('criticality')}"
    reactions = body.get("reaction", [])
    assert reactions, "No reaction block returned"
    assert reactions[0].get("severity") == "mild", f"severity mismatch: {reactions[0].get('severity')}"

tests.name: test_allergy_conflict_rule_no_allergy_no_alert
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <fixture: clinician_token>
A unique patient resource exists via <helper_fn: create_patient()>
No AllergyIntolerance recorded for this patient
actions: POST /MedicationRequest for Amoxicillin without prior allergy for the patient
Verify response contains NO conflict alert or detectedIssue
expected: No conflict alert in response body
Confirms DT row: allergy=absent, medication=present → no alert
trace: R-HLT-034: subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict (contrapositive: no allergy → no warning)
R-HLT-034: alerts regenerated only when conflict exists
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_allergy_conflict_rule_no_allergy_no_alert(clinician_token, create_patient, cleanup_resource):
    patient_id = create_patient(clinician_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    med_payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "723", "display": "Amoxicillin"}]}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/MedicationRequest", json=med_payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    med_id = body["id"]
    cleanup_resource(f"{BASE}/MedicationRequest/{med_id}", headers)
    has_alert = (
        any("detectedIssue" in str(ext) for ext in body.get("extension", []))
        or "detectedIssue" in body
        or body.get("_conflictAlert") is not None
    )
    assert not has_alert, f"Unexpected conflict alert when no allergy recorded: {body}"
